title: arXiv Query: search_query=&id_list=2402.06600&start=0&max_results=1
summary: We prove the completeness of a first-order analogue of the Fischer Servi logic $\mathsf{FS}$ with respect to its expected birelational semantics. To this end we introduce the notion of the $\textit{trace model}$ and, much like in a canonical model argument, prove a truth lemma. We conclude by examining a number of other first-order Fischer Servi logics, including the first-order analogue of $\mathsf{FSS4}$, whose completeness can be similarly proved.
